answers: An IPO (Initial Public Offering) is when a private company offers its shares to the public for the first time on a stock exchange to raise capital.
IPO holo jokhon ekta private company first time public e share sell kore stock exchange e. Era taka raise kore business expand korar jonno.
To invest in an IPO, you can apply through your brokerage account during the subscription period. Research the company's financials and growth potential before investing.
IPOs can offer early investors great returns if the company performs well, but they also carry risks since the stock price can be volatile in the early trading days.
